skill.name: nft-standards
skill.description: Implement NFT smart contract standards including ERC-721, ERC-1155, metadata, royalties, soulbound tokens, and marketplace integration patterns.
skill.summary: Guide NFT contract design with ERC-721, ERC-1155, metadata, royalties, and marketplace patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 38 potential issues, but review found they are false positives from Solidity examples and Markdown code fences. No prompt injection, malicious intent, network exfiltration, environment access, or executable command behavior was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Solidity require Statements Misclassified as Dynamic Require
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static analyzer flagged Solidity require calls as dynamic module loading. The cited lines are contract validation checks inside Solidity examples, not JavaScript require calls or variable imports.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited location is inside a Solidity code block and uses require(...) for runtime contract validation. No dynamic module path, JavaScript loader, or executable script is present.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static analyzer flagged Markdown backticks and fenced code block delimiters as Ruby or shell execution. These delimit Solidity and JSON examples or mark a resource path in prose.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited backticks are Markdown formatting for examples and an inline file path. They do not invoke a shell, interpolate user input, or run commands.

low_findings.title: Keyword-Based Malware and Crypto Flags Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Static high-risk keyword hits occur in NFT standards prose, Solidity interface names, metadata examples, comments, and validation statements. No command-and-control behavior, weak cryptographic implementation, or reconnaissance logic was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file contains educational smart contract snippets and marketplace guidance only. There is no network access, environment access, process execution, or obfuscated payload in the cited context.

content.value_statement: NFT contracts often fail because metadata, minting, royalties, and marketplace behavior are handled inconsistently. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through practical ERC-721 and ERC-1155 implementation patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains ERC-721 minting patterns with supply limits, pricing, token URIs, and owner withdrawals.
Shows ERC-1155 multi-token patterns for game items, batch minting, supply tracking, and burning.
Describes off-chain IPFS metadata and on-chain metadata generation concepts.
Covers EIP-2981 royalty interfaces and configurable royalty recipient logic.
Includes examples for soulbound tokens, dynamic NFTs, and ERC721A gas-optimized minting.
Lists marketplace compatibility considerations for major NFT marketplaces.
limitations: The repository snapshot contains only SKILL.md, so referenced resource and asset files are not available.
Examples are educational and require a full Solidity security audit before production deployment.
OpenZeppelin APIs change over time, so imports and overrides may need version-specific updates.
The skill does not deploy contracts, verify contracts, or manage private keys.

use_cases.title: Plan a Collectible NFT Contract
use_cases.description: Define ERC-721 supply rules, mint pricing, token URI strategy, and withdrawal behavior before implementation.

use_cases.title: Design Game Item Tokens
use_cases.description: Use ERC-1155 patterns to model fungible and non-fungible game assets with per-item supply limits.

use_cases.title: Prepare Marketplace Metadata
use_cases.description: Structure token metadata, royalties, and marketplace integration requirements for an NFT launch.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Token Standard
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose between ERC-721 and ERC-1155 for this NFT project: [describe assets, supply, transfers, and marketplace goals].

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Minting Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Design the minting strategy for an NFT collection with these constraints: [supply, price, allowlist, reveal plan, and royalty needs].

prompt_templates.title: Review Metadata Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my NFT metadata approach for marketplace compatibility, IPFS usage, attributes, image handling, and reveal workflow: [paste description].

prompt_templates.title: Compare Advanced Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare soulbound, dynamic NFT, royalty, and ERC721A patterns for this product design. Identify tradeoffs, risks, and verification steps.

output_examples.input: I need an NFT contract for 10,000 collectibles with paid public minting.
output_examples.output: A concise ERC-721 plan covering supply caps, per-mint limits, payment validation, token URI generation, owner withdrawal, and required test cases.

output_examples.input: Our game has swords, shields, and potions with different supplies.
output_examples.output: An ERC-1155 design that maps item IDs to supply limits, supports owner minting, batch minting, burning, and metadata URI planning.

output_examples.input: We need royalties and marketplace compatibility for an NFT launch.
output_examples.output: A checklist for EIP-2981 support, royalty fee bounds, recipient management, metadata fields, IPFS pinning, and marketplace-specific review items.

best_practices: Use audited base contracts such as OpenZeppelin or ERC721A, then pin exact dependency versions.
Separate product requirements from contract logic before writing mint, metadata, and royalty behavior.
Run Solidity tests, static analysis, and an independent audit before mainnet deployment.
anti_patterns: Do not treat example contracts as production-ready without dependency updates and security review.
Do not store mutable metadata without a documented reveal, freeze, or governance policy.
Do not design royalties, mint limits, or owner controls without explaining marketplace and user trust impacts.

faq.question: Does this skill support both ERC-721 and ERC-1155?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance and examples for single-token NFTs and multi-token collections.

faq.question: Can it create production-ready smart contracts?
faq.answer: It can guide implementation, but all contracts need testing, dependency review, and security audit before deployment.

faq.question: Does it include NFT metadata guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers IPFS metadata, on-chain metadata concepts, attributes, images, and dynamic metadata patterns.

faq.question: Does it cover royalty standards?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes EIP-2981 royalty behavior and configurable royalty recipient patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with soulbound tokens?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a non-transferable token pattern with minting and owner-controlled burning concepts.

faq.question: Are the referenced resource files included?
faq.answer: No. This audited snapshot contains only SKILL.md, so referenced resource and asset files are unavailable.
